name: Cyber Security
description: A specialized program focusing on protecting computer systems, networks, and data from security threats. Covers cryptography, ethical hacking, forensics, and security governance.

subjects.name: Cryptography
subjects.description: Study of cryptographic techniques for secure communication including symmetric and asymmetric encryption, hash functions, digital signatures, key exchange protocols, and cryptographic standards.
learning_outcomes: Implement symmetric encryption algorithms including AES and DES
Apply asymmetric cryptography using RSA and ECC
Design secure key exchange and digital signature schemes
Analyze cryptographic protocols for vulnerabilities
subjects.industry_relevance: Cryptography secures all modern digital communications including e-commerce, banking, messaging, and blockchain systems.

subjects.name: Ethical Hacking
subjects.description: Hand-on course covering ethical hacking methodologies, reconnaissance, scanning, exploitation, post-exploitation, and reporting. Includes practical use of Kali Linux and Metasploit.
learning_outcomes: Perform reconnaissance and information gathering using OSINT tools
Exploit vulnerabilities using Metasploit and custom exploits
Conduct wireless network attacks and web application attacks
Document and present penetration testing findings professionally
subjects.industry_relevance: Ethical hackers are in high demand to identify vulnerabilities before malicious actors exploit them across all industries.

subjects.name: Network Security
subjects.description: Security of computer networks covering firewalls, IDS/IPS, VPNs, secure protocols, network segmentation, wireless security, and defense against network-level attacks.
learning_outcomes: Configure firewalls and network access control policies
Implement VPNs using IPSec and SSL/TLS
Deploy intrusion detection and prevention systems
Mitigate DDoS attacks and network-based threats
subjects.industry_relevance: Network security is critical for protecting enterprise infrastructure, data centers, and cloud environments.

subjects.name: Information Security
subjects.description: Comprehensive overview of information security principles including CIA triad, risk management, security policies, compliance frameworks (ISO 27001, NIST), and security governance.
learning_outcomes: Apply CIA triad principles to information security scenarios
Conduct risk assessments and develop risk mitigation strategies
Implement security policies aligned with ISO 27001 and NIST frameworks
Design business continuity and disaster recovery plans
subjects.industry_relevance: Information security is essential for regulatory compliance (GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS) in finance, healthcare, and government sectors.

subjects.name: Malware Analysis
subjects.description: Study of malware types, analysis techniques, reverse engineering, and threat intelligence. Covers static and dynamic analysis, debugging, unpacking, and malware detection methods.
learning_outcomes: Perform static analysis of malware using disassemblers and decompilers
Conduct dynamic analysis in sandboxed environments
Reverse engineer packed and obfuscated malware samples
Write YARA rules for malware detection and classification
subjects.industry_relevance: Malware analysis is critical for incident response teams, antivirus companies, and threat intelligence organizations.

subjects.name: Digital Forensics
subjects.description: Principles and practices of digital forensic investigation covering evidence collection, disk forensics, memory forensics, network forensics, mobile device forensics, and legal procedures.
learning_outcomes: Acquire and preserve digital evidence following chain of custody
Perform disk forensics analysis using tools like Autopsy and EnCase
Conduct memory forensics to detect rootkits and hidden processes
Prepare forensic reports admissible in legal proceedings
subjects.industry_relevance: Digital forensics is vital for law enforcement, corporate investigations, and incident response teams handling security breaches.

subjects.name: Penetration Testing
subjects.description: Advanced penetration testing methodologies covering web application testing, network penetration testing, social engineering, wireless attacks, and reporting with OWASP and PTES frameworks.
learning_outcomes: Conduct web application penetration tests following OWASP methodology
Exploit Active Directory and internal network vulnerabilities
Perform social engineering engagements and physical security assessments
Write professional penetration testing reports with remediation recommendations
subjects.industry_relevance: Penetration testers are essential for organizations to validate security controls and comply with security standards like PCI-DSS.

subjects.name: Secure Coding
subjects.description: Principles and practices for writing secure code. Covers common vulnerabilities (OWASP Top 10), input validation, authentication, session management, secure cryptography usage, and code review.
learning_outcomes: Identify and mitigate OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities in source code
Implement secure authentication and authorization mechanisms
Apply input validation and output encoding to prevent injection attacks
Conduct security code reviews using SAST tools
subjects.industry_relevance: Secure coding practices are mandatory in regulated industries and are increasingly required in SDLC processes across all software companies.

subjects.name: Cloud Security
subjects.description: Security considerations for cloud computing environments including shared responsibility model, identity and access management, cloud compliance, container security, and serverless security.
learning_outcomes: Design cloud security architectures using IAM policies and security groups
Implement encryption for data at rest and in transit in cloud environments
Secure containerized workloads using image scanning and runtime protection
Apply zero-trust principles to cloud infrastructure
subjects.industry_relevance: Cloud security expertise is critical as organizations migrate workloads to AWS, Azure, and GCP at accelerating pace.

subjects.name: Blockchain Security
subjects.description: Security aspects of blockchain technology covering smart contract vulnerabilities, consensus mechanism attacks, DeFi security, wallet security, and blockchain forensics.
learning_outcomes: Identify smart contract vulnerabilities including reentrancy and integer overflow
Audit Solidity smart contracts for security issues
Analyze consensus mechanism attacks like 51% attacks
Conduct blockchain forensics for cryptocurrency transaction tracing
subjects.industry_relevance: Blockchain security is increasingly important with the growth of DeFi, NFTs, and enterprise blockchain adoption.
